text.title: TTS Implementation — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every production TTSService must enforce: cache identity, eviction, chunk isolation, semantic split, async-safe streaming, clone consent.

rule.statement: TTSService cache key MUST include `provider`. The canonical form is `sha256(text + "|" + voice + "|" + str(speed) + "|" + provider).hexdigest()`. Provider is identity, not configuration.

rationale.source: Cross-provider audio comparison: OpenAI nova vs Google en-US-Neural2-F vs ElevenLabs Rachel produce non-interchangeable output

rule.rationale: Same text + same semantic voice on a different provider produces audibly different audio. Without provider in the key, a pipeline that switches providers serves stale cached audio under the new provider's name.

rule.statement: Implement cache eviction by both max_age_days (default 30) and max_size_mb (default 500). Run eviction on a schedule (cron, before each synthesis batch) — never rely on disk to fill before alerting.

rationale.source: Production disk-fill incidents on long-running media pipelines

rule.rationale: TTS audio caches accumulate gigabytes silently. A 6-month-old pipeline with no eviction fills the host disk and breaks the service with ENOSPC mid-batch.

rule.statement: LongTextTTS MUST use `tempfile.mkdtemp()` for chunk paths. Never use predictable paths like `/tmp/chunk_{i}.mp3`. Cleanup with `shutil.rmtree(tmpdir, ignore_errors=True)` after assembly.

rationale.source: Concurrent batch synthesis failure traces (multiple agents on same host)

rule.rationale: Two LongTextTTS instances writing to `/tmp/chunk_0.mp3` simultaneously corrupt each other's outputs. mkdtemp() makes paths non-overlapping by construction.

rule.statement: Split long text on semantic boundaries (paragraphs, headings) before sentence-level splitting. Reject inputs with no sentence-ending punctuation (`.!?`) by chunk_size — they will produce one oversized chunk that exceeds the API limit.

rationale.source: LongTextTTS regex `(?<=[.!?])\s+` failures on code-block / bulleted / data-dump payloads

rule.rationale: Code blocks and bulleted lists have no sentence punctuation. The naive regex returns the whole input as one chunk, then the API rejects it at 4096+ chars and the entire long-form synthesis fails.

rule.statement: stream_tts() MUST be an async generator yielding bytes. Sync callers MUST bridge via `asyncio.run(collect_stream(...))`. Never call `response.stream_to_file()` directly inside an async coroutine without `asyncio.to_thread()`.

rationale.source: Event-loop block warnings under load (FastAPI + LiveKit)

rule.rationale: stream_to_file is sync I/O that blocks asyncio's event loop for the full streaming window. Other voice turns stall mid-conversation until the file write completes.

rule.statement: ElevenLabs clone_voice() MUST be preceded by a consent record lookup. The record contains `{speaker_id, signed_at, scope, sample_paths[]}`. Without a valid record matching the requested scope, the call is rejected with `status="error", error="missing consent"`.

rationale.source: ElevenLabs ToS Section 4.3 (Voice Cloning); legal exposure on unauthorized voice clones

rule.rationale: Cloning a person's voice without consent violates ElevenLabs ToS and in many jurisdictions is unauthorized commercial use of likeness. Hard-coding the gate makes the policy enforceable in code review.
